name.description: The name of the skill to activate.

functionDeclarations.name: analyze_multimedia
functionDeclarations.description:
Analyzes images, videos, and audio files using a multimodal model. This tool accepts local file paths, base64 data, or URLs to multimedia content. Supported formats include common image types (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, BMP), video formats (MP4, MKV, OGG), and audio formats (MP3, WAV, FLAC, OGG, M4A).

Key capabilities:
• Visual Agent: Operates PC/mobile GUIs, recognizes UI elements, understands functions, can guide through interface interactions
• Visual Coding: Generates Draw.io diagrams, HTML/CSS/JS code from images/videos
• Text Extraction: Reads and extracts all text from images including documents, receipts, screenshots
• Spatial Perception: Judges object positions, viewpoints, occlusions, provides 2D grounding
• Content Analysis: Describes scenes, identifies objects, analyzes actions in videos
• Audio Understanding: Transcribes speech, identifies sounds, analyzes audio content
• Technical Understanding: Interprets charts, diagrams, technical drawings, code screenshots

The model responds based on your specific prompt. Examples: 'Extract all text from this receipt', 'Generate HTML code for this UI design', 'Identify all clickable elements in this screenshot', 'Describe the spatial relationships between objects', 'Transcribe the speech in this audio', 'What sounds are in this recording?'.

content.description: The multimedia content. For 'absolute_path': provide the absolute file path. For 'base64': provide base64 data (with or without 'data:mime/type;base64,' prefix). For 'url': provide the complete URL starting with http:// or https://.

prompt.description: The prompt or question to ask about the multimedia content. Be specific about what you want to know. Examples: 'Describe what you see in this image', 'Extract all text from this receipt', 'Generate HTML/CSS code for this UI design', 'Identify all clickable elements and their functions in this screenshot', 'What actions are happening in this video?', 'Create a Draw.io diagram based on this flowchart image', 'Describe the spatial relationships between objects', 'What UI components are visible and how can I interact with them?', 'Transcribe the speech in this audio file', 'What sounds can you hear in this recording?', 'Identify the speakers in this conversation'.

functionDeclarations.name: enter_plan_mode
functionDeclarations.description: Request approval to proactively enter Plan Mode for non-trivial implementation work. Use it when the task needs structured planning before coding, but not for simple fixes or pure research. This requires user approval and is only available in interactive sessions.

functionDeclarations.name: execute_csharp_script
functionDeclarations.description: Executes a C# script in Unity Editor using Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Scripting. Captures and returns logs generated during script execution. The C# scripting package must be available in Unity. Supports inline script code or loading from a file path.

script.description: C# script code to execute OR absolute path to a .cs file. If a file path is provided (e.g., "/path/to/script.cs"), Unity will automatically detect it and read the file content. PREFERRED for executing existing script files. The script runs in the Unity Editor C# environment with access to UnityEngine and UnityEditor namespaces.

capture_logs.description: Whether to capture Unity logs during script execution. Default: true. When enabled, all logs (Log, Warning, Error, Exception) generated during execution are captured and returned.

functionDeclarations.name: execute_custom_tool
functionDeclarations.description: Executes a custom Unity tool by name. Custom tools are registered in Unity Editor via C# attributes or tool providers. Use this for user-defined or plugin-provided tools not covered by built-in Unity tools.

tool_name.description: Name of the custom tool to execute. Must match a registered tool name in Unity.

parameters.description: Parameters to pass to the custom tool. Structure depends on the specific tool.

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when finding files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 1000). Use to limit output for broad patterns.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

file_filtering_options.description: Optional: Whether to respect ignore patterns from .gitignore or .geminiignore

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when listing files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

respect_gemini_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .geminiignore patterns when listing files. Defaults to true.

functionDeclarations.name: lsp
functionDeclarations.description: Interact with LSP servers for code intelligence: definition, references, hover, symbols, implementation, and call hierarchy.

filePath.description: Absolute or relative file path in workspace.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: read_many_files
functionDeclarations.description:
Reads content from multiple files specified by paths or glob patterns within a configured target directory. For text files, it concatenates their content into a single string. It is primarily designed for text-based files. However, it can also process image (e.g., .png, .jpg) and PDF (.pdf) files if their file names or extensions are explicitly included in the 'paths' argument. For these explicitly requested non-text files, their data is read and included in a format suitable for model consumption (e.g., base64 encoded).

This tool is useful when you need to understand or analyze a collection of files, such as:
- Getting an overview of a codebase or parts of it (e.g., all TypeScript files in the 'src' directory).
- Finding where specific functionality is implemented if the user asks broad questions about code.
- Reviewing documentation files (e.g., all Markdown files in the 'docs' directory).
- Gathering context from multiple configuration files.
- When the user asks to "read all files in X directory" or "show me the content of all Y files".

Use this tool when the user's query implies needing the content of several files simultaneously for context, analysis, or summarization. For text files, it uses default UTF-8 encoding and a '--- {filePath} ---' separator between file contents. Ensure paths are relative to the target directory. Glob patterns like 'src/**/*.js' are supported. Avoid using for single files if a more specific single-file reading tool is available, unless the user specifically requests to process a list containing just one file via this tool. Other binary files (not explicitly requested as image/PDF) are generally skipped. Default excludes apply to common non-text files (except for explicitly requested images/PDFs) and large dependency directories unless 'useDefaultExcludes' is false.

paths.description: Required. An array of glob patterns or paths relative to the tool's target directory. Examples: ['src/**/*.ts'], ['README.md', 'docs/']

include.description: Optional. Additional glob patterns to include. These are merged with `paths`. Example: "*.test.ts" to specifically add test files if they were broadly excluded.

exclude.description: Optional. Glob patterns for files/directories to exclude. Added to default excludes if useDefaultExcludes is true. Example: "**/*.log", "temp/"

recursive.description: Optional. Whether to search recursively (primarily controlled by `**` in glob patterns). Defaults to true.

useDefaultExcludes.description: Optional. Whether to apply a list of default exclusion patterns (e.g., node_modules, .git, binary files). Defaults to true.

file_filtering_options.description: Whether to respect ignore patterns from .gitignore or .geminiignore

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when listing files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

respect_gemini_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .geminiignore patterns when listing files. Defaults to true.

functionDeclarations.name: replace
functionDeclarations.description:
Replaces text within a file. Use this tool for targeted edits to existing files.

The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. The `old_string` MUST uniquely identify the specific instance to change — unless `replace_all` is set to true, in which case every occurrence of `old_string` will be replaced. Use the read_file tool to establish the current file state before the first text replacement. If the tool reports that the file changed since it was read, re-read it before retrying.

If you need to replace the full contents of an existing file, use `write_file` instead.
If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.
Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

Expectation for required parameters:
1. `file_path` MUST be an absolute path; otherwise an error will be thrown.
2. Prefer exact literal text for `old_string`, including relevant surrounding context and matching whitespace, indentation, and newlines when possible.
3. Prefer exact literal text for `new_string`, and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.
4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

**Important:** Exact literal matches are the most reliable. The tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping, but do not rely on fallback matching when you can provide the exact text. The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use `replace_all` to change every instance of `old_string`.
Use `replace_all` for replacing and renaming strings across the file. This parameter is useful if you want to rename a variable for instance.

file_path.description: The absolute path to the file to modify. Must start with '/'.

old_string.description: Prefer exact literal text when possible, preferably unescaped. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation as closely as you can. The `old_string` should uniquely match one location in the file unless `replace_all` is true. Exact matches are the most reliable, and the tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping when needed. If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.

new_string.description: Prefer exact literal text to replace `old_string` with, preferably unescaped. Provide the intended final text and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences of old_string (default false). Use this for replacing and renaming strings across the file.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact command to execute using the best available Windows shell (automatically selected from PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt). Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

run_in_background.description: Set to true to run this command in the background. Returns immediately with task metadata and an output file path.

functionDeclarations.name: save_memory
functionDeclarations.description:
Saves a specific piece of information or fact to your long-term memory.

Use this tool:

- When the user explicitly asks you to remember something (e.g., "Remember that I like pineapple on pizza", "Please save this: my cat's name is Whiskers").
- When the user states a clear, concise fact about themselves, their preferences, or their environment that seems important for you to retain for future interactions to provide a more personalized and effective assistance.

Do NOT use this tool:

- To remember conversational context that is only relevant for the current session.
- To save long, complex, or rambling pieces of text. The fact should be relatively short and to the point.
- If you are unsure whether the information is a fact worth remembering long-term. If in doubt, you can ask the user, "Should I remember that for you?"

## Parameters

- `fact` (string, required): The specific fact or piece of information to remember. This should be a clear, self-contained statement. For example, if the user says "My favorite color is blue", the fact would be "My favorite color is blue".
- `scope` (string, optional): Where to save the memory:
  - "global": Saves to user-level ~/.codely-cli/CODELY.md (shared across all projects)
  - "project": Saves to current project's CODELY.md (project-specific)
  - If not specified, the tool will ask the user where they want to save the memory.

fact.description: The specific fact or piece of information to remember. Should be a clear, self-contained statement.

scope.description: Where to save the memory: "global" saves to user-level ~/.codely-cli/CODELY.md (shared across all projects), "project" saves to current project's CODELY.md (project-specific). If not specified, will prompt user to choose.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. Returns the lines containing matches, along with their file paths and line numbers.

pattern.description: The regular expression (regex) pattern to search for within file contents (e.g., 'function\s+myFunction', 'import\s+\{.*\}\s+from\s+.*').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

include.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 20, max: 150). Values above 150 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches.

functionDeclarations.name: unity_asset
functionDeclarations.description:
Manage Unity project assets on disk (under "Assets/") such as materials, physics materials, textures/importers, ScriptableObjects, and existing prefabs.

Key uses:
- Read: search assets, get info (optionally with preview), get components from a Prefab asset
- Write: create folders/materials/physics materials/scriptable objects; modify existing assets (including setting Prefab component properties); move/rename/duplicate assets; ensure .meta health

Important notes:
- `create` currently supports only: Folder, Material, PhysicsMaterial, ScriptableObject (Prefab creation is not supported).
- `rename` is effectively a move: pass a full destination asset path (e.g. "Assets/Foo/NewName.ext").
- `generate_preview` can return large base64 payloads; avoid unless you truly need a thumbnail.

Asset (re)import operations are heavy and should only be used when absolutely necessary. Prefer normal Unity import, ensure_*, and script recompilation instead of full reimport.

action.description:
Operation to perform.

Notes:
- `ensure_has_meta` verifies a .meta exists (generates via safe reimport if missing).
- `ensure_meta_integrity` checks meta health and returns recommendations (no auto-fix).
- `rename` behaves like `move` (destination is a full asset path, not just a name).
- `create` supports only: Folder, Material, PhysicsMaterial, ScriptableObject.
- `modify` can edit Prefab assets by setting component properties (see `properties`).

mode.description: Batch mode: 'stop_on_error' stops at first failure (default). 'continue_on_error' runs all ops and collects failures.

ops.description: Batch operations (server-side). Each op runs sequentially inside Unity. Constraints: max 10 ops. Use create_batch for write-only sequences (no reads). Use edit_batch for "search-then-write" edits: one or more search ops with captureAs (must match <= 1 asset; 0 matches => early-stop success), followed by write ops that use the captured $alias as their path.

params.description: Parameters for the op action (same shape as unity_asset, minus action).

allowFailure.description: If true, this op failure won't stop execution even in stop_on_error mode.

captureAs.description: edit_batch only: for search ops, capture the resolved asset path into a $alias (e.g. '$asset') for later write ops.

path.description:
Asset path or scope.
- For most actions, pass a Unity asset path under `Assets/` (e.g. "Assets/Materials/MyMaterial.mat").
- For `search`, `path` is treated as a folder scope (e.g. "Assets/" or "Assets/MyFolder").
- For batch actions, paths are typically provided per-op in `ops[].params.path`.

asset_type.description:
Asset type (e.g., 'Material', 'Folder') - required for `create`.
NOTE: `create` currently supports only: Folder, Material, PhysicsMaterial, ScriptableObject.

assetType.description: Alias of asset_type (camelCase). Prefer this in prompts; will be sent as asset_type to Unity.

properties.description:
Dictionary of properties for "create" "modify".
example properties for Material: {"color": [1, 0, 0, 1], "shader": "Standard"}.
example Material texture assignment: {"texture": {"name": "_MainTex", "path": "Assets/Textures/T.png"}}.
example properties for Texture: {"width": 1024, "height": 1024, "format": "RGBA32"}.
example properties for PhysicsMaterial: {"bounciness": 1.0, "staticFriction": 0.5, "dynamicFriction": 0.5}.
example Prefab component modification (Prefab asset only): {"MyComponent": {"someField": 123, "enabled": true}}.
Tip: when setting UnityEngine.Object fields (e.g., Texture, Material), pass an asset path string; Unity will load and assign it.

shader.description: Shader name for materials (e.g., "Standard", "Universal Render Pipeline/Lit")

color.description: RGBA color values [r, g, b, a] where each component is 0-1

frictionCombine.description: How friction values are combined in physics materials

bounceCombine.description: How bounce values are combined in physics materials

scriptClass.description: Class name for ScriptableObject creation (required for ScriptableObject type)

destination.description: Destination asset path under "Assets/" for duplicate/move/rename (rename is a move to the new path). Example: "Assets/NewFolder/NewName.mat".

generate_preview.description: Generate preview for the asset (may include large base64 PNG). Use sparingly.

filter_type.description: Filter by asset type for search operations

functionDeclarations.name: unity_bake
functionDeclarations.description: Handles NavMesh and Lighting baking operations. Supports async operation tracking for long-running bake processes with configurable timeouts. Can also clear baked NavMesh and lighting data.

async.description: For bake_navmesh: use async UpdateNavMesh instead of synchronous BuildNavMesh. Requires existing NavMesh data on surfaces.

op_id.description: Operation ID returned from bake_navmesh/bake_lighting (required for wait_for_bake)

functionDeclarations.name: unity_console
functionDeclarations.description: Gets messages from or clears the Unity Editor console. Supports filtering by message type, text content, timestamp, and formatting options. Clear action returns a sinceToken for incremental reads until Unity publishes a newer state.

scope.description: Scope for clear action: "all" clears everything (default), "errors_only" clears only error messages

count.description: Maximum number of messages to return

filterText.description: Text filter for messages (case-insensitive substring match)

since_token.description: Token from previous clear operation for incremental reading. Valid until Unity publishes a newer state; if invalidated, clear/read again for a fresh boundary. Use this instead of sinceTimestamp.

sinceTimestamp.description: (DEPRECATED) Get messages after this timestamp (ISO 8601 format). Use since_token instead for reliable incremental reads.

functionDeclarations.name: unity_editor
functionDeclarations.description: Controls Unity Editor state (play/pause/stop), retrieves project information and full UnityCurrentState, manages editor tools, windows, selection, tags, and layers. Use get_current_state for comprehensive state snapshot.

action.description: Action to perform. Use "get_current_state" for full state, "start_compilation_pipeline" for script changes, "focus_window" to focus a specific editor window.

op_id.description: Operation ID returned from request_compile (required for wait_for_compile)

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for wait operations (default: 60 for compile, 600 for idle)

waitForCompletion.description: Wait for action to complete before returning

tagName.description: Tag name for add_tag or remove_tag actions

layerName.description: Layer name for add_layer or remove_layer actions

windowType.description: Window type for focus_window action. Common types: Console, Inspector, Hierarchy, Project, Scene, Game, Animator, Animation, Profiler

functionDeclarations.name: unity_gameobject
functionDeclarations.description: Manages Unity GameObjects with comprehensive operations including create, modify, delete, find, list_children, and component management. Supports primitives, prefabs, transforms, and advanced component manipulation with property setting and reference assignments.

action.description: Operation to perform. Ensure operations are idempotent (safe to call multiple times). `set_component_properties` is an alias of `set_component_property` (recommended for batch property sets). Use `create_batch` for write-only deterministic batches (no reads); use `edit_batch` for "find-then-write" edits with an early-stop when 0 targets are found. Use "list_children" to retrieve a depth-limited *tree-structured* children hierarchy for a specific GameObject (large results can be written to a JSON file under Assets/). Use "select" to set Unity Editor selection. For full-scene hierarchy roots, prefer unity_scene action="get_hierarchy".

mode.description: Batch mode: 'stop_on_error' stops at first failure (default). 'continue_on_error' runs all ops and collects failures.

ops.description: Batch operations (server-side). Each op runs sequentially inside Unity. Constraints: max 10 ops. Use create_batch for write-only sequences (no reads). Use edit_batch for "find-then-write" edits: one or more find ops with captureAs (must match <= 1 target; 0 matches => early-stop success), followed by write ops that target the captured $alias (no by_name targeting in write phase).

params.description: Parameters for the op action (same shape as unity_gameobject, minus action).

allowFailure.description: If true, this op failure won't stop execution even in stop_on_error mode.

captureAs.description: Optional alias (e.g. '$root') to bind this op's primary GameObject instanceID (typically from create) for later refs.

component_type.description: Component type name for ensure_component action

componentType.description: Alias of component_type (camelCase). Prefer this in prompts; will be sent as component_type to Unity.

material.description: Material asset path or GUID for ensure_renderer_material action

mesh.description: Mesh asset path or GUID for ensure_mesh_collider_mesh action

prefab.description: Prefab asset path or GUID for ensure_prefab_default_sprite action

sprite.description: Sprite asset path or GUID for ensure_prefab_default_sprite action

target.description: GameObject identifier (name/path/instance ID) for modify/delete/component actions. Prefer `targetRef` for structured targeting. For list_children, this is the root GameObject whose subtree will be returned.

targetRef.description: Preferred structured reference for the target GameObject. If provided, the tool will derive target+searchMethod. Fields: { id?: number, name?: string, hierarchy_path?: string }.

searchMethod.description: How to find objects. "by_name" (default): match GameObject name. "by_id": match instance ID. "by_path": match hierarchy path (e.g. "Parent/Child"). "by_tag": find by Unity tag. "by_layer": find by layer name or index. "by_component": find objects with specific component type.

name.description: GameObject name - used for both "create" (initial name) and "modify" (rename)

tag.description: Tag name - used for both "create" (initial tag) and "modify" (change tag)

parent.description: Parent GameObject reference - used for both "create" (initial parent) and "modify" (change parent)

rotation.description: Rotation as [x, y, z] Euler angles

componentsToAdd.description: List of component names to add

saveAsPrefab.description: Save the created GameObject as a prefab

prefabFolder.description: Folder for prefab if path not specified

layer.description: Layer name - used for both "create" (initial layer) and "modify" (change layer)

componentsToRemove.description: List of component names to remove

componentProperties.description: Dict mapping Component names to their properties to set. Example: {"Rigidbody": {"mass": 10.0, "useGravity": true}}. To set references: Use asset path string for Prefabs/Materials, e.g., {"MeshRenderer": {"material": "Assets/Materials/MyMat.mat"}}. Use a dict for scene objects/components, e.g.: {"MyScript": {"otherObject": {"find": "Player", "method": "by_name"}}} (assigns GameObject), {"MyScript": {"playerHealth": {"find": "Player", "component": "HealthComponent"}}} (assigns Component). Example set nested property: Access shared material: {"MeshRenderer": {"sharedMaterial.color": [1, 0, 0, 1]}}. For action="set_component_property", you can also pass a *flat* object of properties (e.g. {"mass": 10}) or a nested object under componentName (e.g. {"Rigidbody": {"mass": 10}}).

findAll.description: Find all matching GameObjects (not just first)

depth.description: For list_children action: how many levels below target to traverse (1 = direct children). The returned result keeps a tree structure (nested "children") up to this depth.

includeInactive.description: For list_children action: whether to include inactive objects in the returned results (alias of searchInactive).

resultMode.description: For list_children action: 'auto' returns inline unless the result exceeds maxInlineItems, then writes the full tree JSON to a file and returns its path; 'inline' errors if too large (no truncation); 'file' always writes JSON to disk and returns its path.

maxInlineItems.description: For list_children action: maximum number of descendant nodes (within depth) allowed for inline output before switching to file (or erroring in inline mode).

outputPath.description: For list_children action with resultMode='file' (or auto overflow): output JSON path under Assets/. The tool returns this path so callers can read the full, untruncated result.

selectAll.description: For select action: select all matching GameObjects (not just first). If false, only the first match is selected.

propertyName.description: Shorthand: property name for set_component_property. Alternative: omit propertyName/propertyValue and provide componentProperties (non-empty object) to set multiple properties at once.

propertyValue.description: Shorthand: property value for set_component_property. Alternative: omit propertyName/propertyValue and provide componentProperties (non-empty object) to set multiple properties at once.

includeNonPublicSerialized.description: If True, includes private fields marked [SerializeField] in component data

functionDeclarations.name: unity_menu
functionDeclarations.description: Execute Unity Editor menu items by their menu path or get available menus. Supports all Unity menu commands with safety blacklisting for dangerous operations. Asset reimport-related menu items (e.g., "Assets/Reimport", "Assets/Reimport All") are extremely heavy and should almost never be used by the model; prefer using dedicated tools (script compile, ensure_*, meta checks) and normal Unity import instead of menu-driven mass reimport.

menu_path.description: Full menu path separated by forward slashes (e.g., "File/Save Project", "GameObject/Create Empty", "Assets/Reimport All"). Can use snake_case or camelCase.

menuPath.description: Alternative camelCase naming for menu_path. Full menu path separated by forward slashes.

action.description: Action to perform: "execute" to run menu item, "get_available_menus" to list available menus

parameters.description: Optional parameters for the menu item (rarely used for most menu commands). Future feature.

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds (future feature, currently not used)

alias.description: Menu path alias for common operations (future feature)

functionDeclarations.name: unity_package
functionDeclarations.description: Manages Unity packages via UPM. Install packages by ID or Git URL with optional version, remove packages, list installed packages, and wait for async package operations to complete.

id_or_url.description: Package identifier (e.g., "com.unity.textmeshpro") or Git URL for install_package action

version.description: Optional version to install (e.g., "1.2.3"). Can also use format "package@version" in id_or_url.

op_id.description: Operation ID returned from install_package/remove_package (required for wait_for_upm)

functionDeclarations.name: unity_refresh
functionDeclarations.description: Re-runs the same reconnect flow as `/upm refresh` by re-reading `.com-unity-codely.json` and reconnecting to the Unity Bridge. Call with `{}` when another Unity builtin tool fails because the Bridge is stale/disconnected, then retry the original Unity tool.

functionDeclarations.name: unity_scene
functionDeclarations.description: Manages Unity scenes: create, load, save, get hierarchy, get active scene info, and build settings. IMPORTANT: Scene files use .unity extension for Unity engine and .scene extension for Tuanjie engine (the tool validates/accepts both). Note: for very large scenes, get_hierarchy may return only scene root objects (roots-only) with hints to drill down using unity_gameobject list_children.

action.description: Scene operation to perform. ensure_scene_open requires full scene path ending with .unity or .scene. get_hierarchy may return roots-only for very large scenes.

name.description: Scene name (without .unity/.scene extension). Used for create, load, save actions.

path.description: For ensure_scene_open: full scene path ending with .unity or .scene (e.g., "Assets/Scenes/MyScene.unity"). For create/load/save: directory path (default: "Assets/Scenes/").

functionDeclarations.name: unity_screenshot
functionDeclarations.description: Captures screenshots from Unity Editor. Supports Game View, Scene View (multi-angle), main/specific camera, asset preview, and UI Toolkit document rendering.

action.description: Screenshot action: "capture_game_view" / "capture" – Game View; "capture_scene_view" / "capture_scene_camera" – Scene View (supports multi-angle via view param); "capture_main_camera" – Camera.main; "capture_specific_camera" – named camera; "capture_asset" – instantiate & render asset(s); "capture_ui_toolkit" – render a .uxml UIDocument.

path.description: [All actions] Optional custom save directory for the output image. Defaults to <ProjectRoot>/screenshots.

filename.description: [All actions] Optional custom filename (with or without .png extension). Auto-generated with action prefix and timestamp when omitted.

width.description: [capture_scene_view | capture_main_camera | capture_specific_camera | capture_asset | capture_ui_toolkit] Output width in pixels. Not applicable to capture_game_view (always uses the actual Game View size). Defaults: cameras use their pixel width; others default to 1024.

height.description: [capture_scene_view | capture_main_camera | capture_specific_camera | capture_asset | capture_ui_toolkit] Output height in pixels. Not applicable to capture_game_view (always uses the actual Game View size). Defaults: cameras use their pixel height; others default to 1024.

camera_name.description: [capture_specific_camera – REQUIRED] Name of the camera GameObject to render. The camera is located by exact name match (case-insensitive).

view.description: [capture_scene_view | capture_asset] Camera direction(s) to render. "cardinal" (default) = front + right + top + iso stitched into a grid; "all" = all 7 directions; any other value renders a single direction. Not applicable to capture_game_view, capture_main_camera, capture_specific_camera, or capture_ui_toolkit.

over_time.description: [capture_game_view | capture_scene_view | capture_main_camera | capture_specific_camera] Multi-frame capture. Format: "N" captures N frames; "NxS" captures N frames with S simulation steps between each. Frames are stitched into a grid. Example: "4x1" = 4 frames, 1 step apart.

orthographic.description: [capture_scene_view | capture_asset] Use orthographic projection instead of perspective (default: false). Not applicable to capture_game_view, capture_main_camera, capture_specific_camera, or capture_ui_toolkit.

assets.description: [capture_asset – REQUIRED] Asset paths to instantiate and render (e.g. ["Assets/Prefabs/Hero.prefab"]). Supports GameObject/Prefab, Mesh, Material, Texture2D, Sprite. Multiple assets are laid out side-by-side and captured together.

scene.description: [capture_asset – optional] Path to a .unity scene file (e.g. "Assets/Scenes/Preview.unity"). When provided the scene is opened additively, assets are staged inside it, and the scene is closed after capture. Without this parameter assets are staged in the currently active scene.

document_path.description: [capture_ui_toolkit – REQUIRED] Path to the .uxml VisualTreeAsset (e.g. "Assets/UI/HUD.uxml"). Requires Unity 2021.2 or newer.

focus_object.description: [capture_scene_view – optional] Frame the Scene View camera on a specific GameObject instead of the whole scene bounds before capturing.

focusobject.description: Hierarchy path of the target GameObject (e.g. "Root/Player"). Segments separated by "/".

index.description: Zero-based sibling index to disambiguate when multiple children share the same name at the final path segment (default: 0).

functionDeclarations.name: unity_script
functionDeclarations.description: Create, read, update, delete, and validate C# scripts in Unity projects. Supports MonoBehaviour, ScriptableObject, Editor scripts, and more.

path.description: Asset path for the script (default: Assets/Scripts, without file name and .cs extension)

contents.description: C# code content for create/update operations

include_diagnostics.description: Include full diagnostics in validation results

edits.description: Text edits to apply (1-indexed line/column positions)

precondition_sha256.description: SHA256 hash of current file content for safe text editing

refresh.description: When to refresh Unity after edits

functionDeclarations.name: unity_shader
functionDeclarations.description: Create, read, update, and delete Unity shader files. Handles CRUD operations for shader files within the Unity project with base64 encoding support.

action.description: Shader operation. detect_render_pipeline returns current SRP. ensure_material_shader_for_srp sets correct shader based on SRP.

name.description: Shader name (without .shader extension) for CRUD operations

contents.description: Shader code content for create/update operations

material_guid.description: Material GUID for ensure_material_shader_for_srp (alternative to path)

shader_for_srp.description: Shader mapping for each SRP. builtin is required as fallback. Example: {"builtin": "Standard", "urp": "Universal Render Pipeline/Lit"}

functionDeclarations.name: unity_ui_toolkit
functionDeclarations.description: Manages Unity UI Toolkit assets including PanelSettings, UXML, and USS files. Supports creating, linking, and ensuring UI Toolkit assets exist.

path.description: Asset path (must start with "Assets/" and end with ".asset" for PanelSettings, ".uxml" for UXML, ".uss" for USS)

uxml.description: UXML file path or GUID for link_uss_to_uxml action (shorthand for uxml_path/uxml_guid)

uxml_path.description: UXML file path for link_uss_to_uxml action

uxml_guid.description: UXML GUID for link_uss_to_uxml action (alternative to uxml_path)

uss.description: USS file path or GUID for link_uss_to_uxml action (shorthand for uss_path/uss_guid)

uss_path.description: USS file path for link_uss_to_uxml action

uss_guid.description: USS GUID for link_uss_to_uxml action (alternative to uss_path)

contents.description: Content for create_uxml or create_uss actions

functionDeclarations.name: unity_workflow
functionDeclarations.description: High-level Unity workflows that compress common multi-step patterns (init session, compile+validate, checkpoint) into a single tool call. Prefer this tool over manual sequences when available.

action.description: Workflow action: 'init_session' (sync state + clear console + idle), 'compile_and_validate' (start compilation pipeline + wait + read new console logs), 'checkpoint' (ensure scene saved + capture screenshot), 'install_package_and_validate' (install UPM package + wait + idle + compile_and_validate).

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for the workflow action (compile waits / idle waits).

id_or_url.description: For install_package_and_validate: package identifier (e.g., "com.unity.inputsystem") or Git URL.

version.description: For install_package_and_validate: optional version (e.g., "1.2.3"). Can also be embedded as "id@version" in id_or_url.

screenshot.description: For checkpoint: whether to capture a screenshot (default: true).

screenshotAction.description: For checkpoint screenshot: which capture mode to use (default: capture_game_view). "capture_game_view"/"capture" – Game View; "capture_scene_view"/"capture_scene_camera" – Scene View; "capture_main_camera" – Camera.main; "capture_specific_camera" – named camera; "capture_asset" – asset preview; "capture_ui_toolkit" – UIDocument.

screenshotPath.description: For checkpoint screenshot: optional output folder path.

functionDeclarations.name: web_fetch
functionDeclarations.description: Fetches content from a specified URL and processes it using an AI model. Takes a URL and a prompt as input. Fetches the URL content, converts HTML to markdown, and returns the content for analysis.

url.description: The URL to fetch content from. Must be a fully-formed valid URL starting with "http://" or "https://" (e.g., "https://example.com").

prompt.description: The prompt describing what information to extract from the fetched content (e.g., "Summarize this page", "Extract all API endpoints").

functionDeclarations.name: web_search
functionDeclarations.description:
Searches the internet/web for information using a search query. Use this tool when you need to search for information online but do NOT have a specific URL. Returns search results with titles, snippets, and source URLs.

When to use:
- User asks to "search the web" or "find information about X"
- You need current information, news, or documentation from the internet
- You need to discover web pages related to a topic
- You have a search query but no specific URL

When NOT to use:
- You already have a specific URL (use web_fetch instead)
- User provides a direct URL to access

Example: web_search(query="latest AI developments")

query.description: The search query to find information on the web.

functionDeclarations.name: write_file
functionDeclarations.description:
Writes content to a specified file in the local filesystem.

      Use this tool to create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file.
      Prefer the `replace` tool for targeted edits to existing files.
      Use `write_file` for new files or full-file rewrites.
      Do not use `replace` with an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

      The user has the ability to modify `content`. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

file_path.description: The absolute path to the file to write to (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported.

content.description: The content to write to the file.

functionDeclarations.name: complete_task
functionDeclarations.description:
Call this tool to submit your final result and complete the task. This is the ONLY way to finish.

This tool must be called ALONE in its turn. Do not call any other tools in the same turn. If you still need another tool, do not call this yet.

Your result MUST be an informative handoff for the caller (main agent), not a one-line summary.

Requirements for result:
- Use 2+ short paragraphs or a short markdown sectioned report
- Include what you did / found and the concrete evidence that supports it
- Include file paths, errors, command outcomes, or other artifacts when relevant
- Do NOT return only a title or a single-line summary
- Keep it concise, but detailed enough that the main agent can act without reopening your full trace
- Choose a status that matches the outcome: use 'success' for fully completed work, 'partial' for meaningful progress with straightforward continuation, 'blocked' for external blockers that you cannot resolve autonomously, and 'failure' for invalid or unrecoverable paths

Good result example:
## Findings
I inspected `packages/core/src/foo.ts` and `packages/cli/src/bar.ts`.
The bug is caused by `baz()` skipping null checks in the headless path.

## Evidence
- `packages/core/src/foo.ts:42` calls `baz(input!)`
- `npm test -- foo` fails with `TypeError: ...`

## Next steps
Update the null guard in `foo.ts` and rerun the focused test.

Good result example (partial completion due to budget):
## Completed
- Inspected `packages/core/src/foo.ts` and confirmed the bug location
- Ran `npm test -- foo` to reproduce the failure

## Findings
- The root cause is a missing null guard in `baz()` at line 42
- The fix requires updating both `foo.ts` and the corresponding test file

## Remaining
- Actual code fix not applied (budget exhausted before edit)
- Test update for the new null guard path

## Blockers
Budget exhausted. The fix is straightforward — apply the null guard and rerun tests.

Bad result example:
## Findings
Investigated the issue.

result.description: Provide a substantive handoff of what you did / found. Do not return only a title or single-line summary; include enough evidence and detail for the main agent to act on directly.

next_steps.description: Actionable next steps, or 'none' if there are no follow-ups.

# Unity Master Skill Library

## 概述

本扩展当前提供 **34 个专业技能 + 12 个子代理**，覆盖关卡抽取、试玩广告制作、游戏开发工作流、开发进化分析、AI 资产生成、AI 资产生产、UI 系统、环境渲染、游戏机制九大领域，为 Unity 开发提供全方位专家指导。同时内置 **40+ 篇** 调试经验文档、试玩广告模板和常用代码模板。

## 技能分类

### 关卡抽取 (Level Extract)

从 Unity 项目中抽取指定关卡，移除网络/广告/统计/存档/远程配置等依赖，生成纯单机试玩广告版本。

| Skill                     | 名称               | 说明                                                                                   |
| ------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `level-extract-planner`   | 关卡抽取规划器     | 根据需求文档分析项目结构，生成模块化执行计划（ExecutionPlan.md），将改造拆解为独立模块 |
| `level-extract-developer` | 关卡抽取开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的 ExecutionPlan.md，逐模块执行改造，每模块通过编译门禁并 git commit  |

**子代理（agents/）**：

| Agent                 | 说明                                        |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `editor-plan-agent`   | 改造规划子代理 - 生成/更新 ExecutionPlan.md |
| `editor-module-agent` | 改造执行子代理 - 执行具体模块并通过编译门禁 |

适用场景：试玩、抽关、关卡抽取、playable ads、微信试玩、抖音试玩、单关卡版本、离线版本

**工作流**：

```text
用户提供需求文档 → level-extract-planner → ExecutionPlan.md → level-extract-developer → 改造完成
```

### 试玩广告制作 (Playable Ad)

| Skill                   | 名称               | 说明                                                                                                         |
| ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `playable-ad-planner`   | 试玩广告规划器     | 根据游戏类型和现有素材，规划 30 秒试玩广告的完整流程（开始页→游戏玩法→结算页），生成资产需求清单和开发教程   |
| `playable-ad-developer` | 试玩广告开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的交接产物，优先使用现有资产，无可用资产时调用 TJGenerator 生成，快速实现 30 秒试玩广告体验 |

### 游戏开发工作流 (Game Dev Workflow)

| Skill                | 名称           | 说明                                                                 |
| -------------------- | -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `game-dev-planner`   | 游戏教程规划器 | 根据游戏类型生成 AI 可执行的开发教程和资产需求清单，交接给 developer |
| `game-dev-developer` | 游戏开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的交接产物，先生成资产再直接开发游戏                |
| `game-dev-debugger`  | 游戏调试执行器 | 检索开发经验知识库，为游戏开发/生成过程中的调试问题提供支持          |

### 开发进化分析 (Game Evolution)

| Skill              | 名称           | 说明                                                                                              |
| ------------------ | -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `log-analyzer`     | 日志分析器     | 从开发会话日志中提取 bug、失败模式和可复用经验，去重后自动生成经验文档写入知识库                  |
| `session-profiler` | 会话性能分析器 | 解析聊天日志，统计工具调用次数与失败率、Skill 激活、Sub-Agent 调度、耗时分布，生成 Profiling 报告 |

### AI 资产生成 (TJGenerators)

集成团结 AI 平台的多模态生成能力，无缝嵌入 Unity 编辑器工作流。

| Skill                            | 名称               | 说明                                                                 |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `generate_3d_model`              | 3D 模型生成        | 生成静态 3D 模型；默认优先 Tripo P1，高精度 / PBR / FBX 场景可切换腾讯混元 3D |
| `generate_animated_character`    | 动画角色生成       | 生成带骨骼动画的 Humanoid 角色                                       |
| `generate_rigged_animated_model` | 绑骨动画模型生成   | 为现有模型绑骨和/或生成动作动画                                      |
| `generate_sprite`                | 精灵生成           | 生成 2D 精灵资产（图标、物品图、角色肖像等）                         |
| `generate_sprite_sequence`       | 精灵序列生成       | 从角色参考图生成 2D 逐帧动画                                         |
| `generate_image`                 | 图片生成           | 生成 Texture2D / PNG 图片资产（概念图、UI 背景等）                   |
| `generate_material`              | 材质生成           | 生成 PBR 表面材质（无缝纹理 PNG + `.mat` 文件）                      |
| `generate_skybox`                | 天空盒生成         | 生成天空盒 Cubemap / Skybox 材质                                     |
| `generate_audio_clip`            | 背景音乐生成       | 生成 BGM 和环境音频片段                                              |
| `generate_sound_effect`          | 音效生成           | 生成一次性音效（枪声、脚步声、爆炸等）                               |
| `generate_terrain`               | 地形生成           | 生成大尺度 Unity Terrain 地形并应用到当前场景                        |
| `generate_video`                 | 视频生成           | 生成视频资产（宣传视频、转场视频、场景视频等）                       |
| `search_assets`                  | 资产搜索下载       | 从云端资产库搜索并下载现成资产（搜索→评估→下载）                     |

**子代理（agents/）**：

| Agent                                  | 说明                                         |
| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `ai-generation-planner`                | AI 生成规划子代理 - 多任务生成请求拆解与执行规划 |
| `3d-model-generator`                   | 3D 模型生成子代理                            |
| `animated-character-generator`         | 动画角色生成子代理                           |
| `audio-generator`                      | 音频生成子代理                               |
| `image-generator`                      | 图片生成子代理                               |
| `material-generator`                   | 材质生成子代理                               |
| `skybox-generator`                     | 天空盒生成子代理                             |
| `sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator` | 精灵及序列生成子代理                         |
| `search-assets`                        | 资产搜索与下载子代理                         |
| `video-generator`                      | 视频生成与 VideoPlayer 配置子代理            |

### AI 资产生产 (AI Asset Production)

面向已有资产的接线、放置、地形/骨骼后处理与场景整合。

| Skill                    | 名称               | 说明                                                                 |
| ------------------------ | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `fbx-humanoid-auto-rig`  | FBX Humanoid 自动绑骨 | 使用 UniRig 为任意人形 FBX 自动生成骨架、Humanoid 命名、Skin 与 UV merge |
| `place_assets_in_scene`  | 场景放置专家       | 将已有资产按自然语言要求放入当前场景，支持 Prefab / Sprite / Audio / Material / TerrainData 等 |

### UI 系统 (UI Systems)

| Skill              | 名称            | 说明                                                  |
| ------------------ | --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `ui-system-router` | UI 系统路由器   | 判断合适的 UI 系统并路由到正确的专业技能              |
| `imgui`            | IMGUI 专家      | 为编辑器扩展和调试工具生成即时模式 GUI 代码           |
| `ugui`             | uGUI 专家       | 理解现有 UI、进行针对性编辑、生成新的 Canvas 层级结构 |
| `ui-toolkit`       | UI Toolkit 专家 | 生成 UXML/USS 文件、Manipulators、处理 UI 运行时绑定  |

### 环境渲染 (Environment & Rendering)

| Skill           | 名称                 | 说明                                            |
| --------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `cinemachine`   | Cinemachine 3.1 专家 | 使用模块化组件系统创建正确的摄像机设置          |
| `lighting`      | Lighting 专家        | 为 Built-in 和 URP 管线创建、配置和排查场景光照 |
| `scene-creator` | Scene Creator 专家   | 以编程方式构建、验证和修复 2D/3D 场景           |

### 游戏机制 (Gameplay Mechanics)

| Skill          | 名称             | 说明                               |
| -------------- | ---------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `2d-character` | 2D 角色专家      | 端到端处理角色创建工作流           |
| `auto-wire`    | 资产自动接线专家 | 自动将生成的资产接入用户项目       |
| `input-system` | 输入系统专家     | 确定合适的系统并实现健壮的输入设置 |

## 经验知识库

### 关卡抽取需求文档 (experience/templates-level-extract/)

| 模板           | 说明                                                                                             |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `LevelExtract` | 关卡抽取试玩广告的标准需求文档模板，包含业务需求约束：纯单机、无存档、无广告、无统计、无远程配置 |

### 试玩广告模板 (experience/templates-playable-ad/)

| 模板                    | 说明                     |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `playable_start_page`   | 标题、按钮动画、场景切换 |
| `playable_guide_system` | 目标点、箭头、完成检测   |
| `playable_end_page`     | 分数展示、重玩按钮、微信通知 |

### 调试经验 (experience/development/)

当前内置 **30+ 篇** Unity 开发调试经验文档，涵盖常见问题：

- **UI 问题**：UGUI 遮挡 Camera、EventSystem 缺失导致点击失效、中文/字体显示异常
- **物理问题**：CapsuleCollider 中心漂移、Collider 未设 `isTrigger`、Player 插入地面
- **编译错误**：DLL 访问冲突、Library 缓存错误、匿名类型数组 `CS0826`、私有方法访问
- **渲染问题**：Sprite 半透明边缘、粒子材质显示方块、Standard Shader 光泽度无效
- **运行时问题**：场景对象被禁用、自动射击未实现、相机跟随抖动、游戏状态管理缺失

### 代码模板 (experience/templates/)

| 模板                 | 说明       |
| -------------------- | ---------- |
| `asset_loader`       | 资产加载器 |
| `camera_follow`      | 相机跟随   |
| `collectible_system` | 收集系统   |
| `procedural_meshes`  | 程序化网格 |
| `ui_panels`          | UI 面板    |

## 使用方式

```bash
# 激活特定技能
activate_skill("<skill-name>")

# 关卡抽取
activate_skill("level-extract-planner")
activate_skill("level-extract-developer")

# 试玩广告
activate_skill("playable-ad-planner")

# 游戏开发工作流

activate_skill("game-dev-developer")
activate_skill("game-dev-debugger")

# AI 资产生成

activate_skill("generate_sprite")
activate_skill("generate_sprite_sequence")

# Unity 专家

activate_skill("cinemachine")
activate_skill("scene-creator")
activate_skill("ui-system-router")
```

## 技能依赖关系

```text
level-extract-planner → level-extract-developer
editor-plan-agent → editor-module-agent (关卡抽取子代理)

playable-ad-planner → playable-ad-developer

ai-generation-planner → 3d-model-generator | animated-character-generator | audio-generator | image-generator | material-generator | skybox-generator | sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator | search-assets | video-generator

generate_3d_model → place_assets_in_scene
generate_animated_character → place_assets_in_scene
generate_sprite → place_assets_in_scene
generate_terrain → place_assets_in_scene
search_assets → search-assets → place_assets_in_scene
generate_video → video-generator

2d-character → auto-wire
ui-system-router → imgui | ugui | ui-toolkit
```

--- Context from: .codely-cli\extensions\unity-master-skill-library\CODELY.md ---
# Unity Master Skill Library

## 概述

本扩展当前提供 **34 个专业技能 + 12 个子代理**，覆盖关卡抽取、试玩广告制作、游戏开发工作流、开发进化分析、AI 资产生成、AI 资产生产、UI 系统、环境渲染、游戏机制九大领域，为 Unity 开发提供全方位专家指导。同时内置 **40+ 篇** 调试经验文档、试玩广告模板和常用代码模板。

## 技能分类

### 关卡抽取 (Level Extract)

从 Unity 项目中抽取指定关卡，移除网络/广告/统计/存档/远程配置等依赖，生成纯单机试玩广告版本。

| Skill                     | 名称               | 说明                                                                                   |
| ------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `level-extract-planner`   | 关卡抽取规划器     | 根据需求文档分析项目结构，生成模块化执行计划（ExecutionPlan.md），将改造拆解为独立模块 |
| `level-extract-developer` | 关卡抽取开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的 ExecutionPlan.md，逐模块执行改造，每模块通过编译门禁并 git commit  |

**子代理（agents/）**：

| Agent                 | 说明                                        |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `editor-plan-agent`   | 改造规划子代理 - 生成/更新 ExecutionPlan.md |
| `editor-module-agent` | 改造执行子代理 - 执行具体模块并通过编译门禁 |

适用场景：试玩、抽关、关卡抽取、playable ads、微信试玩、抖音试玩、单关卡版本、离线版本

**工作流**：

```text
用户提供需求文档 → level-extract-planner → ExecutionPlan.md → level-extract-developer → 改造完成
```

### 试玩广告制作 (Playable Ad)

| Skill                   | 名称               | 说明                                                                                                         |
| ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `playable-ad-planner`   | 试玩广告规划器     | 根据游戏类型和现有素材，规划 30 秒试玩广告的完整流程（开始页→游戏玩法→结算页），生成资产需求清单和开发教程   |
| `playable-ad-developer` | 试玩广告开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的交接产物，优先使用现有资产，无可用资产时调用 TJGenerator 生成，快速实现 30 秒试玩广告体验 |

### 游戏开发工作流 (Game Dev Workflow)

| Skill                | 名称           | 说明                                                                 |
| -------------------- | -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `game-dev-planner`   | 游戏教程规划器 | 根据游戏类型生成 AI 可执行的开发教程和资产需求清单，交接给 developer |
| `game-dev-developer` | 游戏开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的交接产物，先生成资产再直接开发游戏                |
| `game-dev-debugger`  | 游戏调试执行器 | 检索开发经验知识库，为游戏开发/生成过程中的调试问题提供支持          |

### 开发进化分析 (Game Evolution)

| Skill              | 名称           | 说明                                                                                              |
| ------------------ | -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `log-analyzer`     | 日志分析器     | 从开发会话日志中提取 bug、失败模式和可复用经验，去重后自动生成经验文档写入知识库                  |
| `session-profiler` | 会话性能分析器 | 解析聊天日志，统计工具调用次数与失败率、Skill 激活、Sub-Agent 调度、耗时分布，生成 Profiling 报告 |

### AI 资产生成 (TJGenerators)

集成团结 AI 平台的多模态生成能力，无缝嵌入 Unity 编辑器工作流。

| Skill                            | 名称               | 说明                                                                 |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `generate_3d_model`              | 3D 模型生成        | 生成静态 3D 模型；默认优先 Tripo P1，高精度 / PBR / FBX 场景可切换腾讯混元 3D |
| `generate_animated_character`    | 动画角色生成       | 生成带骨骼动画的 Humanoid 角色                                       |
| `generate_rigged_animated_model` | 绑骨动画模型生成   | 为现有模型绑骨和/或生成动作动画                                      |
| `generate_sprite`                | 精灵生成           | 生成 2D 精灵资产（图标、物品图、角色肖像等）                         |
| `generate_sprite_sequence`       | 精灵序列生成       | 从角色参考图生成 2D 逐帧动画                                         |
| `generate_image`                 | 图片生成           | 生成 Texture2D / PNG 图片资产（概念图、UI 背景等）                   |
| `generate_material`              | 材质生成           | 生成 PBR 表面材质（无缝纹理 PNG + `.mat` 文件）                      |
| `generate_skybox`                | 天空盒生成         | 生成天空盒 Cubemap / Skybox 材质                                     |
| `generate_audio_clip`            | 背景音乐生成       | 生成 BGM 和环境音频片段                                              |
| `generate_sound_effect`          | 音效生成           | 生成一次性音效（枪声、脚步声、爆炸等）                               |
| `generate_terrain`               | 地形生成           | 生成大尺度 Unity Terrain 地形并应用到当前场景                        |
| `generate_video`                 | 视频生成           | 生成视频资产（宣传视频、转场视频、场景视频等）                       |
| `search_assets`                  | 资产搜索下载       | 从云端资产库搜索并下载现成资产（搜索→评估→下载）                     |

**子代理（agents/）**：

| Agent                                  | 说明                                         |
| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `ai-generation-planner`                | AI 生成规划子代理 - 多任务生成请求拆解与执行规划 |
| `3d-model-generator`                   | 3D 模型生成子代理                            |
| `animated-character-generator`         | 动画角色生成子代理                           |
| `audio-generator`                      | 音频生成子代理                               |
| `image-generator`                      | 图片生成子代理                               |
| `material-generator`                   | 材质生成子代理                               |
| `skybox-generator`                     | 天空盒生成子代理                             |
| `sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator` | 精灵及序列生成子代理                         |
| `search-assets`                        | 资产搜索与下载子代理                         |
| `video-generator`                      | 视频生成与 VideoPlayer 配置子代理            |

### AI 资产生产 (AI Asset Production)

面向已有资产的接线、放置、地形/骨骼后处理与场景整合。

| Skill                    | 名称               | 说明                                                                 |
| ------------------------ | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `fbx-humanoid-auto-rig`  | FBX Humanoid 自动绑骨 | 使用 UniRig 为任意人形 FBX 自动生成骨架、Humanoid 命名、Skin 与 UV merge |
| `place_assets_in_scene`  | 场景放置专家       | 将已有资产按自然语言要求放入当前场景，支持 Prefab / Sprite / Audio / Material / TerrainData 等 |

### UI 系统 (UI Systems)

| Skill              | 名称            | 说明                                                  |
| ------------------ | --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `ui-system-router` | UI 系统路由器   | 判断合适的 UI 系统并路由到正确的专业技能              |
| `imgui`            | IMGUI 专家      | 为编辑器扩展和调试工具生成即时模式 GUI 代码           |
| `ugui`             | uGUI 专家       | 理解现有 UI、进行针对性编辑、生成新的 Canvas 层级结构 |
| `ui-toolkit`       | UI Toolkit 专家 | 生成 UXML/USS 文件、Manipulators、处理 UI 运行时绑定  |

### 环境渲染 (Environment & Rendering)

| Skill           | 名称                 | 说明                                            |
| --------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `cinemachine`   | Cinemachine 3.1 专家 | 使用模块化组件系统创建正确的摄像机设置          |
| `lighting`      | Lighting 专家        | 为 Built-in 和 URP 管线创建、配置和排查场景光照 |
| `scene-creator` | Scene Creator 专家   | 以编程方式构建、验证和修复 2D/3D 场景           |

### 游戏机制 (Gameplay Mechanics)

| Skill          | 名称             | 说明                               |
| -------------- | ---------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `2d-character` | 2D 角色专家      | 端到端处理角色创建工作流           |
| `auto-wire`    | 资产自动接线专家 | 自动将生成的资产接入用户项目       |
| `input-system` | 输入系统专家     | 确定合适的系统并实现健壮的输入设置 |

## 经验知识库

### 关卡抽取需求文档 (experience/templates-level-extract/)

| 模板           | 说明                                                                                             |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `LevelExtract` | 关卡抽取试玩广告的标准需求文档模板，包含业务需求约束：纯单机、无存档、无广告、无统计、无远程配置 |

### 试玩广告模板 (experience/templates-playable-ad/)

| 模板                    | 说明                     |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `playable_start_page`   | 标题、按钮动画、场景切换 |
| `playable_guide_system` | 目标点、箭头、完成检测   |
| `playable_end_page`     | 分数展示、重玩按钮、微信通知 |

### 调试经验 (experience/development/)

当前内置 **30+ 篇** Unity 开发调试经验文档，涵盖常见问题：

- **UI 问题**：UGUI 遮挡 Camera、EventSystem 缺失导致点击失效、中文/字体显示异常
- **物理问题**：CapsuleCollider 中心漂移、Collider 未设 `isTrigger`、Player 插入地面
- **编译错误**：DLL 访问冲突、Library 缓存错误、匿名类型数组 `CS0826`、私有方法访问
- **渲染问题**：Sprite 半透明边缘、粒子材质显示方块、Standard Shader 光泽度无效
- **运行时问题**：场景对象被禁用、自动射击未实现、相机跟随抖动、游戏状态管理缺失

### 代码模板 (experience/templates/)

| 模板                 | 说明       |
| -------------------- | ---------- |
| `asset_loader`       | 资产加载器 |
| `camera_follow`      | 相机跟随   |
| `collectible_system` | 收集系统   |
| `procedural_meshes`  | 程序化网格 |
| `ui_panels`          | UI 面板    |

# Unity Master Skill Library

## 概述

本扩展当前提供 **34 个专业技能 + 12 个子代理**，覆盖关卡抽取、试玩广告制作、游戏开发工作流、开发进化分析、AI 资产生成、AI 资产生产、UI 系统、环境渲染、游戏机制九大领域，为 Unity 开发提供全方位专家指导。同时内置 **40+ 篇** 调试经验文档、试玩广告模板和常用代码模板。

## 技能分类

### 关卡抽取 (Level Extract)

从 Unity 项目中抽取指定关卡，移除网络/广告/统计/存档/远程配置等依赖，生成纯单机试玩广告版本。

| Skill                     | 名称               | 说明                                                                                   |
| ------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `level-extract-planner`   | 关卡抽取规划器     | 根据需求文档分析项目结构，生成模块化执行计划（ExecutionPlan.md），将改造拆解为独立模块 |
| `level-extract-developer` | 关卡抽取开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的 ExecutionPlan.md，逐模块执行改造，每模块通过编译门禁并 git commit  |

**子代理（agents/）**：

| Agent                 | 说明                                        |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `editor-plan-agent`   | 改造规划子代理 - 生成/更新 ExecutionPlan.md |
| `editor-module-agent` | 改造执行子代理 - 执行具体模块并通过编译门禁 |

适用场景：试玩、抽关、关卡抽取、playable ads、微信试玩、抖音试玩、单关卡版本、离线版本

**工作流**：

```text
用户提供需求文档 → level-extract-planner → ExecutionPlan.md → level-extract-developer → 改造完成
```

### 试玩广告制作 (Playable Ad)

| Skill                   | 名称               | 说明                                                                                                         |
| ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `playable-ad-planner`   | 试玩广告规划器     | 根据游戏类型和现有素材，规划 30 秒试玩广告的完整流程（开始页→游戏玩法→结算页），生成资产需求清单和开发教程   |
| `playable-ad-developer` | 试玩广告开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的交接产物，优先使用现有资产，无可用资产时调用 TJGenerator 生成，快速实现 30 秒试玩广告体验 |

### 游戏开发工作流 (Game Dev Workflow)

| Skill                | 名称           | 说明                                                                 |
| -------------------- | -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `game-dev-planner`   | 游戏教程规划器 | 根据游戏类型生成 AI 可执行的开发教程和资产需求清单，交接给 developer |
| `game-dev-developer` | 游戏开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的交接产物，先生成资产再直接开发游戏                |
| `game-dev-debugger`  | 游戏调试执行器 | 检索开发经验知识库，为游戏开发/生成过程中的调试问题提供支持          |

### 开发进化分析 (Game Evolution)

| Skill              | 名称           | 说明                                                                                              |
| ------------------ | -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `log-analyzer`     | 日志分析器     | 从开发会话日志中提取 bug、失败模式和可复用经验，去重后自动生成经验文档写入知识库                  |
| `session-profiler` | 会话性能分析器 | 解析聊天日志，统计工具调用次数与失败率、Skill 激活、Sub-Agent 调度、耗时分布，生成 Profiling 报告 |

### AI 资产生成 (TJGenerators)

集成团结 AI 平台的多模态生成能力，无缝嵌入 Unity 编辑器工作流。

| Skill                            | 名称               | 说明                                                                 |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `generate_3d_model`              | 3D 模型生成        | 生成静态 3D 模型；默认优先 Tripo P1，高精度 / PBR / FBX 场景可切换腾讯混元 3D |
| `generate_animated_character`    | 动画角色生成       | 生成带骨骼动画的 Humanoid 角色                                       |
| `generate_rigged_animated_model` | 绑骨动画模型生成   | 为现有模型绑骨和/或生成动作动画                                      |
| `generate_sprite`                | 精灵生成           | 生成 2D 精灵资产（图标、物品图、角色肖像等）                         |
| `generate_sprite_sequence`       | 精灵序列生成       | 从角色参考图生成 2D 逐帧动画                                         |
| `generate_image`                 | 图片生成           | 生成 Texture2D / PNG 图片资产（概念图、UI 背景等）                   |
| `generate_material`              | 材质生成           | 生成 PBR 表面材质（无缝纹理 PNG + `.mat` 文件）                      |
| `generate_skybox`                | 天空盒生成         | 生成天空盒 Cubemap / Skybox 材质                                     |
| `generate_audio_clip`            | 背景音乐生成       | 生成 BGM 和环境音频片段                                              |
| `generate_sound_effect`          | 音效生成           | 生成一次性音效（枪声、脚步声、爆炸等）                               |
| `generate_terrain`               | 地形生成           | 生成大尺度 Unity Terrain 地形并应用到当前场景                        |
| `generate_video`                 | 视频生成           | 生成视频资产（宣传视频、转场视频、场景视频等）                       |
| `search_assets`                  | 资产搜索下载       | 从云端资产库搜索并下载现成资产（搜索→评估→下载）                     |

**子代理（agents/）**：

| Agent                                  | 说明                                         |
| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `ai-generation-planner`                | AI 生成规划子代理 - 多任务生成请求拆解与执行规划 |
| `3d-model-generator`                   | 3D 模型生成子代理                            |
| `animated-character-generator`         | 动画角色生成子代理                           |
| `audio-generator`                      | 音频生成子代理                               |
| `image-generator`                      | 图片生成子代理                               |
| `material-generator`                   | 材质生成子代理                               |
| `skybox-generator`                     | 天空盒生成子代理                             |
| `sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator` | 精灵及序列生成子代理                         |
| `search-assets`                        | 资产搜索与下载子代理                         |
| `video-generator`                      | 视频生成与 VideoPlayer 配置子代理            |

### AI 资产生产 (AI Asset Production)

面向已有资产的接线、放置、地形/骨骼后处理与场景整合。
